summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app to a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching, then iteratively build out the extension with sessions list, session detail, checkpoint sidebar, MCP server, search, image rendering, agent-aware commands, and packaging.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with: sessions list with name/slug display, session detail with compact full-width message rows, native checkpoint TreeView sidebar with file diffs, MCP server embedded at /mcp, QuickPick search with FTS, image rendering in session detail, agent-aware labels and terminal commands, VSIX packaging script, and onboarding flow for new users.

repo: Server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (the worktree), not gossamer/dist/serve.js — all server rebuilds must happen in the worktree and source files must be manually synced there
Each project in ~/.gossamer/config.json has its own dbPath — never create a root-level database
Custom session names (/rename) produce custom-title events only in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/*.jsonl, not in shadow or checkpoint branches
Session slug (auto-generated) lives on LogEvent.slug and never changes on rename; custom-title events are the true rename signal
Entire.io writes to entire/checkpoints/v1 branch; shadow branch captures live session events; neither branch contains image content or custom-title events
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on entire/checkpoints/v1 branch and must be backfilled from LogEvent.data JSON column for pre-schema rows
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview — must store as window.__vscodeApi and reuse
VS Code webview CSP requires explicit img-src data: https: http: rule for base64 image rendering
VS Code TreeView resourceUri requires absolute paths (not relative) to show SCM file decorations
The server's /api/branch-log endpoint requires prefix-matching (findRepoForPath) not exact match because session cwd may be a subdirectory of the registered repo root
Session checkpoints from the server were ordered asc by createdAt — needed desc for newest-first display
Entire.io tags new commits to the same session ID even after the agent stops — use session updatedAt as the boundary for Session vs Branch History sections
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin stores entire/checkpoints/v1 OIDs which may not be in the working repo — commit messages must be looked up in the branch-log as a fallback
The gossamer.vsix and bundled-server/ are generated artifacts that should not be committed
DEFAULT_PORT = 3456 is stored in src/config.ts and used across server, VS Code extension, and install-mcp script for stable MCP configuration

code.finding: Must use include: ['src/**/*.ts'] not specific file list — omitting files causes them to never be compiled, producing silent runtime failures (SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were missing from dist)
Server is a static singleton (static server: ChildProcess, static serverPort) so it survives panel disposal; spawnServer() reuses existing static server if port matches
getSystemNode() uses execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get system node, NOT process.execPath which points to VS Code's bundled Electron node — critical for native module compatibility
onDidChangeViewState listener must call checkpointProvider.setSession() on e.webviewPanel.active to update sidebar when switching between existing session tabs
Must check window.__vscodeApi before calling acquireVsCodeApi() since GossamerPanel injects it as a pre-acquired global to avoid the one-call-per-webview limit

File watchers (fs.watch) replace 500ms DB poll and 5s shadow poll — DB watches .db + .db-wal + parent dir (50ms debounce), git watches .git/ non-recursively + .git/refs/ recursively (200ms debounce), both with 30s fallback polls

Auto-scroll to bottom must use useEffect([loading]) after loading becomes false, not setTimeout(0) — the timeout fires before React flushes the render to DOM; must also check distFromBottom < 80 to avoid overriding user scroll position
Branch history commitMessage lookup: session checkpoints may have null commitMessage because their gitOid is not in the working repo — fall back to the branchLogMap built from /api/branch-log results
SERVE_SCRIPT uses existsSync fallback: checks bundled-server/serve.js first (VSIX install), then ../../../dist/serve.js (dev mode)

workflow: Gossamer-testing worktree pattern: edit in gossamer/, manually sync changed files with cp, rebuild in gossamer-testing/ — same branch cannot be checked out in two worktrees so a testing branch was created from mcp
VS Code extension development: F5 launches extension dev host with launch.json args pointing to ~/gossamer as workspace; must have packages/vscode as active workspace
When webview shows stale UI after server rebuild: the webview bundle in gossamer-testing may not have been rebuilt — check if the source files were synced before rebuilding
Gossamer: Restart Server command is needed because the server is a static singleton that survives panel disposal — it won't restart automatically on reload
Prisma schema migrations for SQLite: use --accept-data-loss for nullable column additions; direct ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN is safer for tables with FTS conflicts
vsce package requires a publisher field in package.json and @vscode/vsce installed; esbuild can bundle the server for VSIX distribution with --external for native modules
MCP HTTP transport (StreamableHTTPServerTransport) with sessionIdGenerator: undefined enables stateless mode for simple POST-based tool calls embedded in the existing HTTP server

friction: Gossamer-testing worktree has independent file copies — every change in the main repo must be manually synced and rebuilt, causing repeated 'still seeing old behavior' debugging cycles
Server running from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js instead of gossamer/dist/ was hard to diagnose — many rebuild-and-test cycles before finding the root cause
acquireVsCodeApi() one-call-per-webview limit caused a cryptic runtime failure when the minimize button inline script and the React app both tried to acquire it
VS Code CSP blocked data: URI images silently — no error in webview console, just blank where images should be
tsconfig.extension.json include-list omission caused diffUtils.ts and SessionDetailPanel.ts to never compile — blank diff views with no useful error message
Prisma schema push failed on entireio_cli.db due to FTS table conflict — had to fall back to direct SQLite ALTER TABLE
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin stores entire/checkpoints/v1 OIDs which are in a separate detached branch, not reachable from the working repo HEAD — git log fails silently and commit messages come back null
Electron app changes were accidentally made when the user asked to make the window minimizable — had to revert all of them
Session detail tab ViewColumn changed multiple times (Beside → One → Three → Two) as the user's mental model of the layout evolved
Stray ~/.gossamer/gossamer.db was created during schema push experimentation — needed manual cleanup
open_items: Session color data (color picker in Claude Code UI) is not stored anywhere in disk-accessible files — cannot be displayed until Claude Code persists it
gossamer.vsix artifact should be added to .gitignore along with packages/vscode/bundled-server/
Toggle panel keybinding (Ctrl+K G) does not fire when webview has keyboard focus — needs investigation or alternative approach
Session slug shown in Name column for unnamed sessions is an auto-generated identifier — consider showing nothing/dash instead of the slug for unnamed sessions
The onboarding panel assumes Homebrew is available for entire installation — needs handling for non-Mac or non-brew users
Image content type is not in the LogContent schema contentType enum — image rows are stored but the contentType filter in queries may exclude them
MCP server embed uses stateless StreamableHTTPServerTransport — if MCP clients need session state this will need to be revisited
CLAUDE.md was written but does not document the gossamer-testing worktree pattern or the file sync workflow
The session resume command assumes specific CLI flags (--resume, --session) per agent — these may change as other agents evolve their CLIs
Custom title refresh currently only runs at startup and after shadow polls — a dedicated file watcher on ~/.claude/projects/ would make renames appear faster
